cards.title: Asthma — Naturopathic Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
Safety First ★
Severe asthma (SpO2 <92%, unable to speak in sentences, accessory muscle use, silent chest) = MEDICAL EMERGENCY. Naturopathic interventions are for stable/mild-moderate asthma as adjuncts — never replace reliever or preventer inhalers.
InterventionMechanismDoseEvidence
MagnesiumBronchial smooth muscle relaxation400mg/day oral; IV in acute (A&E)★★★★ Strong (IV acute); moderate (oral prevention)
Vitamin D3Immune modulation, reduces exacerbations2000–4000 IU/day★★★ Good — most asthmatics deficient
Fish oil (EPA+DHA)Anti-inflammatory, reduces LTB43g/day★★★ Good
Boswellia serrata5-lipoxygenase inhibitor — reduces leukotrienes300–400mg TDS★★★ Good (similar to montelukast in mild asthma)
Buteyko breathingRaises CO2, reduces hyperventilationDaily practice★★★ Good — reduces rescue inhaler use
Elimination dietRemove food triggersIdentify: sulphites, salicylates, dairy★★ Moderate — 75% have food triggers
HEPA filterRemove allergens (dust mite, pollen, mould)Bedroom filter★★★ Good for allergic asthma
🟡 Buteyko Breathing
The Buteyko method teaches nasal breathing, breath holding (control pause), and CO2 tolerance. Goal: increase control pause from typical asthmatic level of 10–15 sec to 40+ sec. Evidence: 2 Cochrane-included RCTs show Buteyko reduces bronchodilator use by 86% and significantly improves quality of life. Practical: teach 3 steps — (1) Always breathe through nose, (2) Tape mouth at night, (3) Practise control pause 3x/day.

cards.title: Allergic Rhinitis — Naturopathic Management
cards._legacyHtml:
InterventionDoseMechanismEvidence
Quercetin500mg BD (start 6 weeks before pollen season)Mast cell stabiliser, inhibits histamine release★★★ Good
Vitamin C2g/dayAntihistamine, anti-inflammatory★★★ Good
Stinging nettle (Urtica dioica)300mg freeze-dried leaf TDSH1 receptor antagonist, COX inhibitor★★ Moderate
Nasal saline irrigationNeti pot or bottle, BDMechanical allergen removal, mucosal hydration★★★★ Strong
ProbioticsLactobacillus rhamnosus 10B CFU/dayTh1/Th2 balance, IgE reduction★★★ Good
Local honey1 tbsp/day (local, raw)Desensitisation to local pollen (theoretical)★ Weak — limited RCT evidence
Turmeric + Boswellia500mg each BDLeukotriene and prostaglandin inhibition★★ Moderate
🟡 Neti Pot Technique
Use sterile/boiled-and-cooled water (never tap water — risk of Naegleria fowleri amoeba, though rare). Add 1/4 tsp non-iodised salt per 250ml water (isotonic). Tilt head 45°, insert spout into upper nostril, breathe through mouth. Solution flows through to lower nostril. Clean neti pot after every use, air-dry. Evidence: as effective as intranasal steroids for mild-moderate allergic rhinitis in some trials.

cards.title: COPD — Naturopathic Adjunct Care
cards._legacyHtml:
COPD is irreversible — focus is slowing progression, reducing exacerbations, improving QoL. Never advise stopping inhalers (SABA, LABA, ICS, LAMA) — these save lives.
InterventionBenefitDose
Smoking cessation ★Single most important intervention — slows FEV1 declineAll cessation methods + behavioural support
Pulmonary rehabilitationReduces exacerbations, improves exercise tolerance and QoL3x/week, 8 weeks minimum
N-Acetyl Cysteine (NAC)Mucolytic, antioxidant, reduces exacerbation frequency600mg BD
Vitamin D3Reduces exacerbation frequency (low Vit D = more exacerbations)2000–4000 IU/day
Breathing exercisesPursed lip breathing (reduces air trapping), diaphragmatic breathingDaily practice
Nutrition supportMalnutrition common — high-protein, calorie-dense foods35–45 kcal/kg/day in underweight COPD
VaccinationsPneumococcal + annual influenza — prevents infective exacerbationsPer national schedule

cards.title: Pranayama — Clinical Protocols for Respiratory Health
cards._legacyHtml:
TechniqueMethodDurationIndicationEvidence
Nadi Shodhana (Alternate Nostril)Close R → inhale L → close L → exhale R → inhale R → exhale L10 min dailyHTN, anxiety, arrhythmia, balancingHRV up, cortisol down, BP down
Bhramari (Humming Bee)Plug ears with thumbs, hum on exhalation5–10 minAnxiety, insomnia, sinus headacheVagal activation, nitric oxide up in sinuses
KapalbhatiRapid forceful exhalations 60–120/min, passive inhalation5 min, increase graduallyObesity, constipation, sluggishness, NAFLDImproves respiratory muscle strength and digestion
Ujjayi (Ocean Breath)Partial glottis constriction, 1:2 inhale:exhale ratio10–15 minInsomnia, anxiety, yoga practice, HTNPNS activation, HRV up
4-7-8 BreathingInhale 4 sec, hold 7 sec, exhale 8 sec4 cycles 2x dailyAcute anxiety, craving management, sleep onsetRapid ANS shift to parasympathetic
Bhastrika (Bellows)Rapid forceful inhalation AND exhalation, 1:13–5 minStable mild asthma, obesity, fatigueIncreases PFT parameters
🟡 Pranayama Contraindications
Kapalbhati and Bhastrika — AVOID in: uncontrolled hypertension, cardiovascular disease, epilepsy, hernia, pregnancy, retinal disorders, acute respiratory infection, recent abdominal surgery, psychiatric conditions. Nadi Shodhana and Ujjayi are safe for almost all patients including elderly and cardiac patients. Always start with gentle techniques before vigorous ones.

cards.title: Chronic Sinusitis & ENT — Naturopathic Protocols
cards._legacyHtml:
ConditionAssessmentNaturopathic ProtocolRefer When
Acute sinusitis (viral)Facial pain, nasal congestion. Bacterial if: >10 days, worsening after 5 days, fever >38.5°C, severe unilateral painSaline nasal irrigation BD, steam inhalation (eucalyptus), bromelain 500mg TDS, Vit C 2g, Echinacea. Avoid antibiotics for viral (90% are viral)Bacterial features, orbital symptoms, immunocompromised
Chronic sinusitisSymptoms >12 weeks. Check: allergy, food intolerance (dairy, gluten), anatomy (CT sinus)Eliminate dairy trial 4 weeks, quercetin 500mg BD, Vit D3, probiotics, nasal saline + budesonide irrigationNasal polyps, fungal sinusitis, anatomical obstruction
Recurrent otitis media (children)Frequent ear infections. Check: dairy allergy, passive smoke, daycareDairy elimination trial, Vit D3, zinc, Echinacea winter prophylaxis, xylitol gumHearing loss, language delay, persistent effusion >3 months
🟡 Nasal Saline Irrigation
Neti pot evidence: as effective as intranasal steroids for mild-moderate allergic rhinitis in some trials. ALWAYS use sterile water (boiled and cooled, or distilled) — never tap water due to rare Naegleria fowleri risk. Recipe: 1/4 tsp non-iodised salt per 250ml sterile water. Clean neti pot after every use, air dry. Technique: tilt head 45°, breathe through mouth, solution flows from upper to lower nostril. BD use during acute sinusitis, OD for maintenance in chronic sinusitis.

cards.title: Post-COVID Respiratory Syndrome
cards._legacyHtml:
Long COVID — Respiratory Manifestations
Persistent respiratory symptoms after COVID-19 (>12 weeks) affect 10–30% of patients. Mechanisms: microthrombi, persistent inflammation, autonomic dysfunction (POTS), deconditioning, mast cell activation.
SymptomMechanismNaturopathic Intervention
Breathlessness (normal SpO2)Autonomic dysfunction, hyperventilation, POTSBreathing retraining (Buteyko/Papworth), POTS management, graded exercise
Fatigue + post-exertional malaiseMitochondrial dysfunction, ME/CFS overlapPacing (energy envelope — do NOT push through PEM), CoQ10 300mg, NAD+ precursors, Mg
Persistent coughAirway hypersensitivity, residual inflammationHoney 1 tbsp ★★★, steam inhalation, Thyme syrup
1
Naturopathic ProtocolOmega-3 3–4g, curcumin 1g BD, quercetin 500mg BD (NLRP3 inflammasome inhibitor). Vit D 4000–6000 IU ★★★. CoQ10 300mg, NAC 600mg BD, B-complex, Mg 400mg. Probiotics: gut dysbiosis persists 6+ months post-COVID. Histamine/mast cell: quercetin + Vit C + DAO enzyme.
2
Yoga-Based Pulmonary RehabPhase 1 (weeks 1–4): Shavasana, Nadi Shodhana 3–5 min. No vigorous asanas. Monitor for PEM. Phase 2 (weeks 4–8): Pawanmuktasana, Bhujangasana, Matsyasana. Phase 3 (weeks 8+): Slow Surya Namaskar. Never push through fatigue — pacing is the cardinal principle in Long COVID.

cards.title: Pulmonary Rehabilitation — Exercise Prescription
cards._legacyHtml:
Pulmonary Rehabilitation — Grade A Evidence for COPD
PR reduces hospitalisation 50%, improves 6MWT, reduces dyspnoea. Stronger evidence than most COPD medications (GOLD guidelines Grade A).
Assessment ToolInterpretation
MRC Dyspnoea Scale 1–51: strenuous only. 3: walks slower than peers. 5: too breathless to dress. PR indicated at MRC ≥3.
6-Minute Walk Test (6MWT)Normal >400m. <350m = significant impairment. 30m improvement = clinically significant.
BODE IndexBMI + Obstruction + Dyspnoea + Exercise capacity. Each point ↑ = 34% ↑ respiratory mortality.
1
Exercise PrescriptionAerobic: start 60% max work rate, 20–30 min. Interval training for severe COPD (1:1 ratio). Resistance training (upper + lower limb). IMT (inspiratory muscle training): 30% MIP, 30 breaths 2×/day ★★. Pursed lip breathing during exercise.
2
Yoga PR ProtocolBhujangasana, Setu Bandha, Matsyasana (chest openers). Diaphragmatic breathing in Makarasana. Anulom-Vilom, Bhramari. Avoid Kapalbhati in acute COPD/asthma. Evidence: yoga-based PR shows significant improvement in 6MWT, FVC, FEV1/FVC (India RCT 2014).

cards.title: Childhood Asthma & Allergy — Naturopathic
cards._legacyHtml:
The Atopic March
Eczema → food allergy → allergic rhinitis → asthma. Interrupting the march with gut microbiome optimisation, early allergen introduction, and omega-3 is the most powerful preventive intervention.
Prevention StrategyEvidenceProtocol
Early allergen introduction (LEAP protocol)★★★ LEAP trial: early peanut from 4–11 months reduces allergy 81%High-risk infants: peanut protein 2g × 3/week from 4–11 months
Probiotics in pregnancy + infancy★★★ LGG reduces eczema 50%, asthma in high-riskMaternal LGG from 36 weeks through breastfeeding. Infant: LGG from birth to 6 months.
Omega-3 in pregnancy★★★ COPSAC: fish oil 2.4g reduces child asthma 30%Maternal omega-3 2–3g from 20 weeks gestation
Vit D in pregnancy★★ Low Vit D = ↑ childhood asthma riskTarget Vit D 100–150 nmol/L in pregnancy. Infant: 400 IU/day from birth (IAP)
1
Childhood Asthma ProtocolOmega-3 ★★★, Vit D 1000–2000 IU ★★★, Mg 100–200mg/day, Quercetin 250–500mg/day (mast cell stabiliser), Butterbur PE 50mg BD (children >6) ★★★. Saline nasal irrigation ★★★. Trigger elimination: dust mites, pet dander, mould, cigarette smoke.

cards.title: Tuberculosis — Nutritional Support Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
TB & Malnutrition — A Vicious Cycle
TB causes malnutrition. Malnutrition causes TB. India has 28% of global TB burden. Nutritional support is evidence-based and improves treatment outcomes.
NutrientRoleEvidenceProtocol
Vitamin DActivates macrophage autophagy → kills M. tuberculosis★★★ Cochrane: accelerates sputum culture conversion50,000 IU weekly or 4000–5000 IU daily during DOTS
ZincT-cell and NK cell function. TB patients characteristically zinc depleted.★★★ Multiple RCTs: zinc + DOTS improves outcomesZinc 25–40mg/day during DOTS. Separate from anti-TB drugs by 2 hours.
Pyridoxine (B6)INH is a pyridoxine antagonist — prevents neuropathy★★★ WHO standard of care25–50mg/day throughout INH. 100mg if malnourished/diabetic/pregnant.
ProteinTB causes 2–3% muscle loss/month. Protein essential for immune cell synthesis.★★★1.2–1.5g/kg/day. Small frequent meals. High-calorie supplement if BMI <18.5.
SeleniumSelenoproteins protect against TB oxidative stress★★100–200mcg/day. Avoid >400mcg.
🟡 Hepatoprotection During DOTS
DOTS causes drug-induced liver injury in 5–28% (higher in India — NAT2 slow acetylator phenotype common). Silymarin 140mg TID ★★ (reduces transaminase elevation during DOTS). NAC 600mg BD. Stop DOTS immediately if: jaundice, nausea + ALT >5× ULN, or any sign of liver failure.

cards.title: Pleural & Interstitial Lung Disease — Reference
cards._legacyHtml:
ConditionKey FeaturesDiagnosisNaturopathic Role
Pleural EffusionDullness to percussion, reduced breath sounds. Stony dull = exudate.CXR (blunting of costophrenic >200ml). POCUS. Light's criteria: exudate vs transudate.Transudate (CCF, hypoalbuminaemia): treat cause. Exudate (TB ★ India, malignancy): medical + naturopathic supportive.
PneumothoraxSudden pleuritic pain + breathlessness. Hyper-resonance. Tension PTX: hypotension + deviated trachea = EMERGENCY.CXR. POCUS (absent lung sliding).BNYS contraindicated active PTX. Post-resolution: Buteyko, avoid Bhastrika/Kapalbhati until healed.
IPFVelcro crackles (bibasal). Clubbing. Restrictive PFTs. Poor prognosis (median survival 3–5 years).HRCT: honeycombing + traction bronchiectasis.NAC 600mg TID ★★. Anti-inflammatory diet, Vit D, omega-3. Pulmonary rehabilitation ★★★. Palliative symptom management.
SarcoidosisBilateral hilar lymphadenopathy. Elevated ACE. Multisystem granulomatous disease.CXR + CT. Serum ACE ↑ 75%. Non-caseating granulomata on biopsy.CAUTION: Do NOT supplement Vit D without checking calcium — sarcoidosis causes hypercalcaemia (unregulated macrophage Vit D activation). Anti-inflammatory diet, omega-3, curcumin.
🟡 Light's Criteria
Exudate if ANY of: (1) Pleural:serum protein >0.5. (2) Pleural:serum LDH >0.6. (3) Pleural LDH >2/3 ULN. Exudate causes: TB (★ India — ADA >40 U/L = 90% sensitivity), malignancy, pneumonia. Transudate: CCF, cirrhosis, nephrotic, hypothyroidism.
